articles.headline: From Cisco to Block, more companies are pointing to AI when unveiling job cuts
articles.summary: A growing number of large employers are citing AI as part of their restructuring rationale, underscoring how automation is reshaping staffing plans and budget priorities. For business leaders, the signal is clear: AI investment is increasingly being funded through headcount reduction and operational streamlining rather than incremental budgets.

articles.headline: UMC unveils 14nm eHV FinFET platform for next-gen smartphone logic
articles.summary: UMC’s new foundry platform sharpens competition in mobile and edge chips by improving power efficiency and density. That matters for companies building smartphones, edge AI devices, and other hardware that depends on tight cost and performance targets.

articles.headline: OpenAI floats idea of global AI governance body with U.S. and China
articles.summary: OpenAI’s call for a governance mechanism involving both the U.S. and China highlights how AI policy is becoming a geopolitical issue, not just a technical one. Companies with cross-border data, model deployment, or vendor exposure should watch for compliance and trade implications.
